de.uka.ilkd.key.strategy.quantifierHeuristics

Class PredictCostProver.Clause

  • java.lang.Object
    • de.uka.ilkd.key.strategy.quantifierHeuristics.PredictCostProver.Clause
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    Iterable<Term>
    Enclosing class:
    PredictCostProver

    private class PredictCostProver.Clause
extends Object
implements Iterable<Term>
    A sat() procedure with back searching

        public Iterator<Term> iterator()

        iterator in interface Iterable<Term>

      • cost

        public long cost()
        Returns:
        0 if this clause is refine to false. 1 if true. Otherwise,return the number of literals it left.

      • firstRefine

        public void firstRefine()
        Refine this clause in two process, first try to refined by itself, @see selfRefine. Second refine this clause by assuming assertLiteras are true

      • refine

        public ImmutableSet<Term> refine(Iterable<? extends Term> assertLits)
        Refine literals in this clause, but it does not change literlas, only return literals that can't be removed by refining

      • selfRefine

        public boolean selfRefine(ImmutableSet<Term> lits)
        This method is used for detect where a clause can be simply refined to to true. And it is implemented like this. Assume that the clause contains two literals Li and Lj. If (!Li->Lj) which is acturally (Li|Lj), is true, and the clasue is true. provedByAnthoer(Lj,!Li) is used to proved (!Li->Lj). Some examples are (!a|a) which is (!!a->a) and (a>=1|a<=0) which is !a>=1->a<=0

      • toString

        public String toString()
        Overrides:
        toString in class Object
